channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Udhayanidhi Stalin urges Tamil Nadu voters to support DMK in Assembly elections
item.description: On March 31, 2026, Tamil Nadu Deputy Chief Minister Udhayanidhi Stalin addressed party members and residents in Tiruvannamalai during the state Assembly election campaign. He emphasised that the elect...

item.title: Helen Zille Highlights Johannesburg’s Infrastructure Problems with Pothole Stunt
item.description: Helen Zille, the DA’s mayoral candidate for Johannesburg, made headlines over the weekend after she waded into a waterlogged pothole on Balders Road in Douglasdale. The stunt was intended to draw atte...

item.title: China and Pakistan to coordinate on Iran amid regional tensions
item.description: On 31st March 2026, Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar travelled to Beijing for a brief but significant visit to discuss the ongoing conflict in Iran. Invited by Chinese Foreign Mini...

item.title: Infrared sauna blankets tested: which models offer the best value and comfort at home in 2026
item.description: This article reviews infrared sauna blankets available in 2026, focusing on models that bring a spa‑style sauna experience into the home at a lower cost than full‑sized cabins. The author, a wellness ...

item.title: Gerard Ross Murder Suspect Dies Before Facing Historical Child Abuse Charges
item.description: Christopher James Bartle, a suspect in both the unsolved 1997 murder of schoolboy Gerard Ross and numerous historical child sexual abuse allegations, has died at the age of 66 before facing trial. Bar...

item.title: Weather Alert: Severe Thunderstorm Watch for Southwestern Ontario
item.description: Environment Canada has issued a severe thunderstorm watch for parts of southwestern Ontario, signalling the potential for heavy rain, strong winds, and frequent lightning. Residents in the affected ar...

item.title: NELFUND Clarifies No Increase in Student Upkeep Allowance, Stays at N20,000 Monthly
item.description: The Nigerian Education Loan Fund (NELFUND) has come out strongly to correct some wrong information wey dey spread about the monthly upkeep allowance for students wey dey benefit from their loan scheme...

item.title: Avi Lewis and the Rising Influence of Third-Worldist Ideology in Canadian Politics
item.description: Avi Lewis was recently elected leader of the NDP, taking over a party reduced to six MPs after recent defections. Despite criticisms that he is niche or overly ideological, Lewis represents a growing ...

item.title: MeerKAT telescope reveals 60 previously unseen cosmic structures between galaxy clusters
item.description: Astronomers using South Africa’s MeerKAT radio telescope have discovered 60 previously unknown cosmic structures in the vast regions between galaxies. The MeerKAT Galaxy Cluster Legacy Survey (MGCLS),...

item.title: BJP’s Assam manifesto includes ₹3,000 monthly support for women, UCC, and job creation plans
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), currently in power in Assam, has unveiled its election manifesto promising to raise the monthly payment under the Orunodoi scheme from ₹1,250 to ₹3,000 if it wins a t...

item.title: Tanzania mourns the passing of long-serving MP and Minister William Lukuvi
item.description: This morning in Tanzania feels heavy as the nation mourns the sudden passing of Honourable William Vangimembe Lukuvi on 25th March 2026. He was a sitting minister and Member of Parliament for Isimani ...

item.title: PMD predicts wet weather with thunderstorms in Karachi and Sindh from April 2-4
item.description: According to the Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD), Karachi and several other districts of Sindh are likely to experience rain and thunderstorms starting Thursday, continuing into the first few...

item.title: Ngorongoro to host 11th Safari Field Challenge for tour guide skills development in 2026
item.description: Ngorongoro Conservation Area Authority (NCAA) is preparing for the 11th edition of the Safari Field Challenge, which is set to happen in 2026. This event aims to give tour guides practical knowledge a...

item.title: Federal Court reviews legal challenge in Adriana Rivas extradition case over alleged Pinochet-era kidnappings
item.description: The Federal Court of New South Wales has revisited the long-running extradition case of Adriana Rivas, a Chilean-Australian woman accused of participating in the kidnapping and torture of seven people...

item.title: The Conversation Retracts Article on the Transatlantic Slave Trade Due to Author Affiliation Issues
item.description: On 31 March 2026, The Conversation announced the retraction of an article entitled 'The transatlantic slave trade is the gravest crime against humanity – why the UN declaration matters'. The retractio...

item.title: Kelly Osbourne shares family's unsuccessful attempts to repurchase their former MTV reality show home
item.description: Kelly Osbourne has disclosed that her family has attempted on ten occasions to buy back the Los Angeles mansion where they filmed the iconic MTV reality show 'The Osbournes', yet the current owners ha...

item.title: Kisii Family Mourns Young Woman Who Died in Accident While Supporting Her Education
item.description: A family from Boochi, within Bomachoge Chache Constituency in Kisii County, is mourning the loss of their daughter who passed away in a tragic accident while travelling to Bomet. The young woman, who ...

item.title: New Drug Enters Late-Stage Trial Showing Potential to Slow ALS Progression
item.description: A new drug called pridopidine is entering a pivotal late-stage clinical trial with the potential to slow the progression of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), the most common form of motor neurone d...

item.title: Nigeria Receives 800 Citizens Deported from Niger Amid Diplomatic Concerns
item.description: Last Sunday, Nigerian authorities for Katsina State recorded the return of 800 Nigerians deported from Niger through the Jibia-Magama border. The deportees included 345 men and 185 women from at least...

item.title: South Africans Want Leaders to Address Jobs, Crime, and Service Delivery as GNU Faces Mixed Reviews
item.description: Thirteen months after the 2024 national election, Afrobarometer's survey shows South Africans are largely in favour of elected officials prioritising citizens' concerns, yet many feel MPs and local co...
